1	import { Schema, Struct } from "effect";
2	import { ProjectId, ThreadId, TrimmedNonEmptyString } from "./baseSchemas";
3	
4	import {
5	  ClientOrchestrationCommand,
6	  OrchestrationGetFullThreadDiffInput,
7	  ORCHESTRATION_WS_METHODS,
8	  OrchestrationGetSnapshotInput,
9	  OrchestrationGetTurnDiffInput,
10	  OrchestrationReplayEventsInput,
11	} from "./orchestration";
12	import {
13	  GitCheckoutInput,
14	  GitCreateBranchInput,
15	  GitCreateWorktreeInput,
16	  GitInitInput,
17	  GitListBranchesInput,
18	  GitPullInput,
19	  GitRemoveWorktreeInput,
20	  GitRunStackedActionInput,
21	  GitStatusInput,
22	} from "./git";
23	import {
24	  TerminalClearInput,
25	  TerminalCloseInput,
26	  TerminalOpenInput,
27	  TerminalResizeInput,
28	  TerminalRestartInput,
29	  TerminalWriteInput,
30	} from "./terminal";
31	import { KeybindingRule } from "./keybindings";
32	import { ProjectSearchEntriesInput, ProjectWriteFileInput } from "./project";
33	import { OpenInEditorInput } from "./editor";
34	
35	// ── WebSocket RPC Method Names ───────────────────────────────────────
36	
37	export const WS_METHODS = {
38	  // Project registry methods
39	  projectsList: "projects.list",
40	  projectsAdd: "projects.add",
41	  projectsRemove: "projects.remove",
42	  projectsSearchEntries: "projects.searchEntries",
43	  projectsWriteFile: "projects.writeFile",
44	
45	  // Shell methods
46	  shellOpenInEditor: "shell.openInEditor",
47	
48	  // Git methods
49	  gitPull: "git.pull",
50	  gitStatus: "git.status",
51	  gitRunStackedAction: "git.runStackedAction",
52	  gitListBranches: "git.listBranches",
53	  gitCreateWorktree: "git.createWorktree",
54	  gitRemoveWorktree: "git.removeWorktree",
55	  gitCreateBranch: "git.createBranch",
56	  gitCheckout: "git.checkout",
57	  gitInit: "git.init",
58	
59	  // Terminal methods
60	  terminalOpen: "terminal.open",
61	  terminalWrite: "terminal.write",
62	  terminalResize: "terminal.resize",
63	  terminalClear: "terminal.clear",
64	  terminalRestart: "terminal.restart",
65	  terminalClose: "terminal.close",
66	
67	  // Server meta
68	  serverGetConfig: "server.getConfig",
69	  serverUpsertKeybinding: "server.upsertKeybinding",
70	} as const;
71	
72	// ── Push Event Channels ──────────────────────────────────────────────
73	
74	export const WS_CHANNELS = {
75	  terminalEvent: "terminal.event",
76	  serverWelcome: "server.welcome",
77	  serverConfigUpdated: "server.configUpdated",
78	} as const;
79	
80	// -- Tagged Union of all request body schemas ─────────────────────────
81	
82	const tagRequestBody = <const Tag extends string, const Fields extends Schema.Struct.Fields>(
83	  tag: Tag,
84	  schema: Schema.Struct<Fields>,
85	) =>
86	  schema.mapFields(
87	    Struct.assign({ _tag: Schema.tag(tag) }),
88	    // PreserveChecks is safe here. No existing schema should have checks depending on the tag
89	    { unsafePreserveChecks: true },
90	  );
91	
92	const WebSocketRequestBody = Schema.Union([
93	  // Orchestration methods
94	  tagRequestBody(
95	    ORCHESTRATION_WS_METHODS.dispatchCommand,
96	    Schema.Struct({ command: ClientOrchestrationCommand }),
97	  ),
98	  tagRequestBody(ORCHESTRATION_WS_METHODS.getSnapshot, OrchestrationGetSnapshotInput),
99	  tagRequestBody(ORCHESTRATION_WS_METHODS.getTurnDiff, OrchestrationGetTurnDiffInput),
100	  tagRequestBody(ORCHESTRATION_WS_METHODS.getFullThreadDiff, OrchestrationGetFullThreadDiffInput),
101	  tagRequestBody(ORCHESTRATION_WS_METHODS.replayEvents, OrchestrationReplayEventsInput),
102	
103	  // Project Search
104	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.projectsSearchEntries, ProjectSearchEntriesInput),
105	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.projectsWriteFile, ProjectWriteFileInput),
106	
107	  // Shell methods
108	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.shellOpenInEditor, OpenInEditorInput),
109	
110	  // Git methods
111	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitPull, GitPullInput),
112	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitStatus, GitStatusInput),
113	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitRunStackedAction, GitRunStackedActionInput),
114	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitListBranches, GitListBranchesInput),
115	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitCreateWorktree, GitCreateWorktreeInput),
116	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitRemoveWorktree, GitRemoveWorktreeInput),
117	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitCreateBranch, GitCreateBranchInput),
118	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitCheckout, GitCheckoutInput),
119	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitInit, GitInitInput),
120	
121	  // Terminal methods
122	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.terminalOpen, TerminalOpenInput),
123	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.terminalWrite, TerminalWriteInput),
124	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.terminalResize, TerminalResizeInput),
125	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.terminalClear, TerminalClearInput),
126	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.terminalRestart, TerminalRestartInput),
127	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.terminalClose, TerminalCloseInput),
128	
129	  // Server meta
130	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.serverGetConfig, Schema.Struct({})),
131	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.serverUpsertKeybinding, KeybindingRule),
132	]);
133	
134	export const WebSocketRequest = Schema.Struct({
135	  id: TrimmedNonEmptyString,
136	  body: WebSocketRequestBody,
137	});
138	export type WebSocketRequest = typeof WebSocketRequest.Type;
139	
140	export const WebSocketResponse = Schema.Struct({
141	  id: TrimmedNonEmptyString,
142	  result: Schema.optional(Schema.Unknown),
143	  error: Schema.optional(
144	    Schema.Struct({
145	      message: Schema.String,
146	    }),
147	  ),
148	});
149	export type WebSocketResponse = typeof WebSocketResponse.Type;
150	
151	export const WsPush = Schema.Struct({
152	  type: Schema.Literal("push"),
153	  channel: TrimmedNonEmptyString,
154	  data: Schema.Unknown,
155	});
156	export type WsPush = typeof WsPush.Type;
157	
158	// ── Union of all server → client messages ─────────────────────────────
159	
160	export const WsResponse = Schema.Union([WebSocketResponse, WsPush]);
161	export type WsResponse = typeof WsResponse.Type;
162	
163	// ── Server welcome payload ───────────────────────────────────────────
164	
165	export const WsWelcomePayload = Schema.Struct({
166	  cwd: TrimmedNonEmptyString,
167	  projectName: TrimmedNonEmptyString,
168	  bootstrapProjectId: Schema.optional(ProjectId),
169	  bootstrapThreadId: Schema.optional(ThreadId),
170	});
171	export type WsWelcomePayload = typeof WsWelcomePayload.Type;
172

18	const RuntimeEventRawSource = Schema.Literals([

## Complete Directory Tree and Analysis Report

### DIRECTORY STRUCTURE

```
packages/
├── shared/
│   ├── package.json

│       ├── git.ts             (git branch utilities)
│       ├── logging.ts         (rotating file logging)
│       ├── shell.ts           (shell PATH extraction)
│       └── Net.ts             (networking service with Effect)
│
└── contracts/
    ├── package.json
    ├── tsconfig.json
    └── src/
        ├── index.ts           (barrel export file)
        ├── baseSchemas.ts     (base Effect schemas)
        ├── model.ts           (AI model configuration)
        ├── provider.ts        (provider session schemas)
        ├── orchestration.ts   (domain models - large file)
        ├── ipc.ts             (native API interface)
        ├── terminal.ts        (terminal session schemas)
        ├── editor.ts          (editor support)
        ├── git.ts             (git RPC schemas)
        ├── keybindings.ts     (keyboard binding schemas)
        ├── server.ts          (server config schemas)
        ├── providerRuntime.ts (provider runtime events)
        ├── ws.ts              (websocket request/response schemas)
        └── (+ .test.ts files)
```

---

### 1. PACKAGES/SHARED - package.json

**File:** `/packages/shared/package.json`

```json
{
  "name": "@t3tools/shared",
  "version": "0.0.0-alpha.1",
  "private": true,
  "type": "module",
  "exports": {
    "./model": {
      "types": "./src/model.ts",
      "import": "./src/model.ts"
    },
    "./git": {
      "types": "./src/git.ts",
      "import": "./src/git.ts"
    },
    "./logging": {
      "types": "./src/logging.ts",
      "import": "./src/logging.ts"
    },
    "./shell": {
      "types": "./src/shell.ts",
      "import": "./src/shell.ts"
    },
    "./Net": {
      "types": "./src/Net.ts",
      "import": "./src/Net.ts"
    }
  },
  "scripts": {
    "prepare": "effect-language-service patch",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit",
    "test": "vitest run"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@t3tools/contracts": "workspace:*",
    "effect": "catalog:"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@effect/language-service": "catalog:",
    "@effect/vitest": "catalog:",
    "@types/node": "catalog:",
    "typescript": "catalog:",
    "vitest": "catalog:"
  }
}
```

**Key Points:**
- **Subpath Exports:** 5 exports (model, git, logging, shell, Net) with TypeScript sources
- **ESM Only:** `"type": "module"`
- **Private Workspace Package**
- **Depends on:** `@t3tools/contracts` (workspace dependency)
- **Uses:** Effect library, Node.js types

}

Simple config extending base, includes src directory.

{
  "name": "@t3tools/contracts",

### 9. Key Dependencies & Patterns

- **Branded Types:** Entity IDs use Effect Schema branding
- **Union Schemas:** Extensive use of Schema.Union for discriminated unions
- **Workspace Dependencies:** shared depends on contracts via workspace:*

---

This is a comprehensive TypeScript monorepo with strong type safety via Effect schemas, clear contract definitions for RPC/IPC, and utility functions in the shared package.

### 1. PACKAGES/SHARED - package.json

**File:** `/packages/shared/package.json`

```json
{
  "name": "@t3tools/shared",

  "scripts": {

    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit",

  }
}
```

**Key Points:**

### 4. PACKAGES/CONTRACTS - package.json

**File:** `/packages/contracts/package.json`

```json
{
  "name": "@t3tools/contracts",
  "version": "0.0.8",
  "private": true,
  "files": ["dist"],
  "type": "module",
  "main": "./dist/index.cjs",
  "module": "./dist/index.mjs",
  "types": "./src/index.ts",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "types": "./src/index.ts",
      "import": "./src/index.ts",
      "require": "./dist/index.cjs"
    }
  },
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "tsdown src/index.ts --format esm,cjs --dts --watch --clean",
    "build": "tsdown src/index.ts --format esm,cjs --dts --clean",
    "prepare": "effect-language-service patch",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit",
    "test": "vitest run"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "effect": "catalog:"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@effect/language-service": "catalog:",
    "@effect/vitest": "catalog:",
    "tsdown": "catalog:",
    "typescript": "catalog:",
    "vitest": "catalog:"
  }
}
```

**Key Points:**
- **Single Export:** Only `.` (root export)

### 7. Export Strategy Summary

**@t3tools/shared:**

**@t3tools/contracts:**

## Complete Directory Structure and Exploration Report

---

### 2. REACT CONTEXT PROVIDERS SETUP

The application does **NOT** use React Context heavily. Instead, it uses:

**Zustand Store (State Management)**
- File: `/src/store.ts`
- Uses `create<AppStore>()` from Zustand
- Exports `useStore` hook
- State persisted to localStorage key: `t3code:renderer-state:v8`
- Includes: projects, threads, threadsHydrated
- StoreProvider wraps with Fragment (minimal overhead)

**Router-Level Wrapping**
File: `/src/router.ts` - The Wrap component:
```typescript
Wrap: ({ children }) =>
  createElement(
    QueryClientProvider,
    { client: queryClient },
    createElement(StoreProvider, null, children),
  )
```
This wraps all routes with:
1. TanStack QueryClientProvider (for data fetching)
2. StoreProvider (Zustand wrapper)

**Toast Provider System**
File: `/src/routes/__root.tsx` - Root layout provides:
- `ToastProvider`
- `AnchoredToastProvider`

---

### 3. HOOKS ORGANIZATION

Located in `/src/hooks/` - Three custom hooks:

1. **useTheme.ts** - Theme management hook
   - Uses `useSyncExternalStore` (React 18.1+)
   - Storage key: `t3code:theme`
   - Options: "light" | "dark" | "system"
   - Detects system dark mode via `window.matchMedia("(prefers-color-scheme: dark)")`
   - Suppresses transitions during theme changes using `.no-transitions` class
   - Returns: `{ theme, setTheme, resolvedTheme }`

2. **useMediaQuery.ts** - Media query hook
   - Takes CSS media query string
   - Returns boolean matches state
   - Updates on window.matchMedia changes

3. **useTurnDiffSummaries.ts** - Diff summary memoization
   - Memoizes turn diff summaries from active thread

---

### 4. COMPONENTS STRUCTURE

**Main Layout Components** (`/src/components/`):
- **Sidebar.tsx** - Main sidebar with thread list
- **ChatView.tsx** - Primary chat interface (213KB - largest component)
- **ChatView.browser.tsx** - Browser-specific chat variant
- **DiffPanel.tsx** - Diff viewer component
- **ThreadTerminalDrawer.tsx** - Terminal UI component
- **BranchToolbar.tsx** - Git branch controls
- **GitActionsControl.tsx** - Git action UI
- **ComposerPromptEditor.tsx** - Prompt editor
- **ProjectScriptsControl.tsx** - Project scripts UI
- **PlanSidebar.tsx** - Plan/sidebar alternative
- **ChatMarkdown.tsx** - Markdown rendering for chat
- **DiffWorkerPoolProvider.tsx** - Web worker provider
- **Icons.tsx** - Icon system

**UI Component Library** (`/src/components/ui/`):
40+ base components using Base UI React and Tailwind CSS including:
- Form inputs: input, textarea, field, fieldset, form
- Dialogs: dialog, alert-dialog, popover, sheet
- Navigation: menu, sidebar, group
- Display: card, badge, alert, empty, skeleton, spinner
- Interaction: button, checkbox, switch, radio-group, toggle-group, select, combobox, autocomplete
- Layout: scroll-area, separator, label
- Feedback: toast, tooltip, kbd

---

### 5. CSS CUSTOM PROPERTIES & THEMING

**File**: `/src/index.css`

**Theme System**:
- Uses Tailwind CSS 4 with `@import "tailwindcss"`
- Uses CSS custom properties for theming
- Light and dark theme variants via `.dark` class
- Implements no-transitions suppression for smooth theme switching

**Color Custom Properties**:

Light mode (`:root`):
```css
--radius: 0.625rem
--background: var(--color-white)
--foreground: var(--color-neutral-800)
--card: var(--color-white)
--popover: var(--color-white)
--primary: oklch(0.488 0.217 264)  /* Purple */
--secondary: --alpha(var(--color-black) / 4%)
--muted: --alpha(var(--color-black) / 4%)
--accent: --alpha(var(--color-black) / 4%)
--destructive: var(--color-red-500)
--border: --alpha(var(--color-black) / 8%)
--input: --alpha(var(--color-black) / 10%)
--ring: oklch(0.488 0.217 264)
--info: var(--color-blue-500)
--success: var(--color-emerald-500)
--warning: var(--color-amber-500)
```

Dark mode (`@variant dark`):
```css
--background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--color-neutral-950) 95%, var(--color-white))
--foreground: var(--color-neutral-100)
--card: color-mix(in srgb, var(--background) 98%, var(--color-white))
--primary: oklch(0.588 0.217 264)  /* Slightly lighter purple */
--border: --alpha(var(--color-white) / 6%)
--input: --alpha(var(--color-white) / 8%)
```

**Radius System**:
```css
--radius-sm: calc(var(--radius) - 4px)
--radius-md: calc(var(--radius) - 2px)
--radius-lg: var(--radius)
--radius-xl: calc(var(--radius) + 4px)
--radius-2xl: calc(var(--radius) + 8px)
--radius-3xl: calc(var(--radius) + 12px)
--radius-4xl: calc(var(--radius) + 16px)
```

**Additional Styling**:
- Custom scrollbar styling (6px width, dark mode adjustments)
- Chat markdown specific styles (.chat-markdown class)
- Diff panel theming (.diff-panel-viewport)
- No-transitions class for theme transitions (`transition-duration: 0s !important`)
- Body noise texture overlay
- Frameless titlebar drag region support

---

### 6. LOCAL STORAGE USAGE

**Files using localStorage**:

1. **useTheme.ts** - Theme preference
   - Key: `t3code:theme`
   - Value: "light" | "dark" | "system"
   - Cross-tab sync via `storage` event listener

2. **appSettings.ts** - Application settings
   - Key: `t3code:app-settings:v1`
   - Stored as JSON with schema validation (Effect)
   - Persists: codexBinaryPath, codexHomePath, confirmThreadDelete, enableAssistantStreaming, codexServiceTier, customCodexModels
   - Cross-tab sync via storage listener

3. **store.ts** - Main application state
   - Key: `t3code:renderer-state:v8`
   - Legacy keys: v7, v6, v5, v4, v3 (cleaned up automatically)
   - Also: "codething:renderer-state:v4", v3, v2, v1
   - Stored data: expandedProjectCwds (Set of project paths)
   - Persisted on every Zustand store change

4. **composerDraftStore.ts** - Composer draft state
   - Uses localStorage for draft persistence

5. **terminalStateStore.ts** - Terminal state
   - Terminal state persistence

---

### 7. MAIN ENTRY POINT

**main.tsx**:
```typescript
import React from "react"
import ReactDOM from "react-dom/client"
import { RouterProvider } from "@tanstack/react-router"
import { createHashHistory, createBrowserHistory } from "@tanstack/react-router"

// Entry point setup:
const history = isElectron ? createHashHistory() : createBrowserHistory()
const router = getRouter(history)  // From router.ts
ReactDOM.createRoot(document.getElementById("root")).render(
  <React.StrictMode>
    <RouterProvider router={router} />
  </React.StrictMode>,
)
```

**router.ts** - Router configuration:
- Uses TanStack Router v1.160+
- Creates router with QueryClient and Zustand StoreProvider
- Context provides queryClient for all routes
- Wrap components: QueryClientProvider → StoreProvider

**Root Route** (`routes/__root.tsx`):
- Global error boundary
- ToastProvider + AnchoredToastProvider
- EventRouter + DesktopProjectBootstrap
- Handles native API initialization check

### 2. REACT CONTEXT PROVIDERS SETUP

The application does **NOT** use React Context heavily. Instead, it uses:

**Zustand Store (State Management)**
- File: `/src/store.ts`
- Uses `create<AppStore>()` from Zustand
- Exports `useStore` hook
- State persisted to localStorage key: `t3code:renderer-state:v8`
- Includes: projects, threads, threadsHydrated
- StoreProvider wraps with Fragment (minimal overhead)

**Router-Level Wrapping**
File: `/src/router.ts` - The Wrap component:
```typescript
Wrap: ({ children }) =>
  createElement(
    QueryClientProvider,
    { client: queryClient },
    createElement(StoreProvider, null, children),
  )
```
This wraps all routes with:
1. TanStack QueryClientProvider (for data fetching)
2. StoreProvider (Zustand wrapper)

**Toast Provider System**
File: `/src/routes/__root.tsx` - Root layout provides:
- `ToastProvider`
- `AnchoredToastProvider`

---

### 3. HOOKS ORGANIZATION

Located in `/src/hooks/` - Three custom hooks:

1. **useTheme.ts** - Theme management hook
   - Uses `useSyncExternalStore` (React 18.1+)
   - Storage key: `t3code:theme`
   - Options: "light" | "dark" | "system"
   - Detects system dark mode via `window.matchMedia("(prefers-color-scheme: dark)")`
   - Suppresses transitions during theme changes using `.no-transitions` class
   - Returns: `{ theme, setTheme, resolvedTheme }`

2. **useMediaQuery.ts** - Media query hook
   - Takes CSS media query string
   - Returns boolean matches state
   - Updates on window.matchMedia changes

3. **useTurnDiffSummaries.ts** - Diff summary memoization
   - Memoizes turn diff summaries from active thread

)

content.thinking:
Now I have a very complete picture of the codebase. Let me design the implementation plan.

## Summary of What I Know

### Existing Theme System
The app already has:
- `apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts` — a `useTheme` hook managing `"light" | "dark" | "system"` with localStorage persistence, cross-tab sync, and DOM class toggle
- `apps/web/src/index.css` — CSS custom properties for light/dark modes using Tailwind 4's `@variant dark` approach


- `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` — A settings page with an "Appearance" section that uses the `useTheme` hook to display light/dark/system options

### What the Task Requires
I need to define a theme type in `packages/shared` with at least 3 built-in themes ("t3-dark", "t3-light", "t3-midnight") that cover colors for background, foreground, accent, sidebar, editor, terminal, and status bar, then create a theme provider and update the `useTheme` hook in the web app to persist the selection and apply CSS custom properties. I also need to build a theme selector UI component.

### Key Constraints
I can't modify the build configuration or add dependencies, both `bun lint` and `bun typecheck` need to pass, and the existing light/dark/system mode needs to stay separate from this new named color theme system.

### Design Decisions

I'm thinking through how these two theme dimensions should work together. The existing `useTheme` hook handles light/dark/system mode, and I'm adding a separate color theme system on top of that. The three built-in themes have names starting with "t3-" which suggests they're part of a specific design system, so I should treat color themes as an independent concern from the light/dark toggle.

The approach I'm settling on is to apply named themes via a `data-theme` attribute on the HTML element, with CSS custom properties defining the color palette for each theme. This needs to work alongside Tailwind's existing `.dark` class and `@variant dark` utilities, so the new system should complement rather than replace the current mechanism.

Given the constraints—no build config changes, no new dependencies—I'll define an `AppTheme` type in a new `packages/shared/theme` subpath with pure TypeScript definitions. The `useTheme` hook will be extended to apply the selected theme's CSS variables to the document root, and the dark/light class will be derived from the theme name itself.

Now I'm realizing the existing `@variant dark` selector in the CSS uses `.dark` and `.dark *` to detect dark mode, so I need to think through how custom themes interact with this. I'm considering two approaches: either named themes replace the light/dark distinction entirely (where `t3-dark` and `t3-midnight` both apply the `.dark` class but override different CSS variables), or themes and light/dark modes operate independently of each other.

The first approach seems simpler and avoids build config changes — I'd apply a `data-theme` attribute to the HTML element and let each theme define its own CSS custom properties, with the light/dark mode baked into the theme name itself.

Defining CSS custom properties...

I'm realizing there's a conflict with how Tailwind's dark variant works — it looks for the `.dark` class in the ancestor chain, so I need to set both the `.dark` class and the `data-theme` attribute to make themed dark modes work properly with Tailwind utilities. So the approach is to keep the existing light/dark mode CSS variables intact, then layer theme-specific overrides using `[data-theme]` selectors for any themes that deviate from the base styles. Each theme would apply both the `.dark` class (to enable Tailwind's dark utilities) and its corresponding `data-theme` attribute, with the CSS variables updating accordingly based on which theme is active.

That way the Tailwind `.dark` class still gets applied correctly, and the color theme CSS variables override the defaults. Since "t3-midnight" is inherently a dark theme, selecting it would automatically trigger dark mode.

But actually, re-reading the requirements, it sounds like the theme system should be the primary way colors are defined — the named themes like "t3-dark", "t3-light", and "t3-midnight" are the whole color system, not just overrides on top of a light/dark toggle. So "t3-dark" is a dark theme, "t3-light" is a light theme, and "t3-midnight" is another dark variant with its own distinct color palette.

The implementation would involve defining the theme type and three built-in themes in the shared package, then creating a hook and component in the web app to manage theme selection with localStorage persistence and CSS custom property application. I'd also need to update the styling to work with this theme system, though there's still a consideration I'm working through about how this integrates with the existing setup.

For the implementation, I'm thinking the existing `:root` and `@variant dark` CSS stays as-is for the default themes, and then I add `[data-theme="t3-midnight"]` selectors to override specific variables when that theme is active.

The cleanest approach would be to have each theme define both a `colors` object with these semantic values for the UI selector swatches, and a `cssVars` mapping that covers all the CSS custom properties we need to override—both the standard Tailwind ones and new semantic-area vars like `--sidebar-bg`, `--editor-bg`, `--terminal-bg`, and `--status-bar-bg` that we'd add to the base stylesheet. Defining the theme interface and built-in theme constants... Continuing with the theme configuration, I'm defining the T3 Midnight variant with a deep navy background and sky blue accents, along with custom CSS variables for precise color control. Finishing the color palette definitions...

I'm realizing there's a problem with how t3-light handles CSS variables—it has an empty object, but when switching from t3-midnight we need to actually remove those overrides rather than just leaving them. The cleanest approach would be to have all three themes explicitly define their CSS variables, then the hook can clear everything first before applying the new theme's values and toggling the dark class. That said, it'd be verbose to spell out all the variables for t3-light and t3-dark when we could just rely on the CSS file, so maybe the trade-off of maintaining empty cssVars objects is worth it.

Actually, thinking about this differently—cssVars should only contain the overrides from the base styles. For t3-midnight, those are the differences from the `.dark` CSS variables in the file. The hook would then clear any previously set inline variables on the document element, apply the new theme's overrides, and handle the dark class toggle. Actually the cleanest: maintain a Set of all CSS var names used across ALL themes, and when switching themes, remove all those vars first. Or, on switch, just iterate over all possible vars and `removeProperty` them.

Actually, the simplest: when applying a new theme, first call `document.documentElement.style.cssText = ""` to clear all inline styles, then apply the new theme's cssVars. But this would also clear other inline styles that might be set elsewhere.

Better: track which vars we've set and clear them. The `useTheme` module can maintain a set of currently-applied vars.

- `t3-dark`

The new themes would include their own light/dark character, so "t3-light" = light, "t3-dark" = dark variant, "t3-midnight" = dark variant with different palette.

**Option C: Extend** - Extend `useTheme` so "t3-dark", "t3-light", "t3-midnight" are valid choices alongside "light", "dark", "system".

Now I need to rework the `useTheme` hook to handle the "light" | "dark" | "system" logic properly.

For backward compatibility with the old localStorage values, I should map the old "dark" and "light" strings to the new theme IDs, though using a new localStorage key might be cleaner to avoid stale values.

Now I'm deciding between two approaches for applying the theme CSS: either defining theme-specific custom properties directly in CSS files using `data-theme` selectors, or having the provider/hook inject them dynamically via JavaScript.

The JavaScript approach makes more sense here since the task specifically asks for theme definitions to live in the shared TypeScript package—that way the actual color values are defined as data structures rather than scattered across CSS files, giving us a single source of truth that both the web app and potentially other consumers can use.

Now I'm realizing there's a tension between applying inline styles to `:root` for theme overrides and still needing the `.dark` class to work with Tailwind's dark mode utilities. Let me think through how these interact...

The hook would need to set the custom properties on `:root`, toggle the `.dark` class when switching to dark mode, and also set `color-scheme` for native browser elements.

Now I'm identifying which CSS custom properties need to be dynamically set based on the theme—the color tokens like background, foreground, card, primary, secondary, and all their variants, plus border, input, ring, and info colors.

Let me build a clean implementation that handles the named themes directly. the new implementation

But I need to make sure I'm actually covering all the surfaces the task requires: the theme type and `BUILT_IN_THEMES` constant need to define values for background, foreground, accent, sidebar, editor, terminal, and status bar across all three built-in themes.

The hook will handle applying these CSS custom properties to the DOM, and the theme selector component will go in the settings page to let users pick between themes and see preview swatches.

Defining the theme interface... Defining the built-in theme objects with their configurations for light, dark, and midnight variants, then creating a resolver function that returns the appropriate theme based on user preference or system settings.

Now I'm working out the actual color values for the theme objects—I need to convert the Tailwind CSS variables into concrete hex values since inline styles can't reference CSS custom properties directly. I'm mapping out the neutral palette and other colors from Tailwind CSS 4 to use in the JavaScript theme definitions. I'm keeping the primary color in oklch format since it's a core design token, then mapping out the rest of the light theme with concrete hex values and rgba equivalents—primaryForeground as white, secondary and accent as subtle black overlays, muted text in a mid-gray, destructive states in red, and borders/inputs with varying opacity levels.

Now I'm defining the dark theme variant with inverted values—a near-black background with light foreground text, darker sidebar and editor backgrounds, a terminal-appropriate dark gray, and a purple accent for the status bar. I've got the color palette locked in for both themes—the lighter purple tones for the first one and the deep navy with blue accents for midnight. Now I'm setting up a hook that applies these colors to the document root by setting CSS custom properties dynamically.

But I'm realizing there's a mismatch between what I want to add and what the existing app actually uses. The sidebar currently relies on `bg-card` rather than a dedicated `bg-sidebar` class, and the whole component structure is built around the original set of CSS variables like `--background` and `--card`. I need to either map the new surface colors to existing class names or update the components to use the new variables—but changing the existing CSS would be a bigger refactor than just adding the theme hook. Still writing CSS mappings...

Now I'm laying out the complete implementation strategy. I need to define the theme types and interfaces in the shared package, set up the subpath export, then refactor the hook to use these centralized definitions.

For the hook itself, I'll replace the current implementation with imports from the shared theme module, switch to the new `ThemePreference` type, and update the localStorage key while handling migration from the old key. The theme application will use CSS custom properties and class toggling, with the same initialization pattern as before.

Adding CSS variables...

Now I'm setting up the ThemeSelector component to display swatches for each built-in theme plus a System option, with each swatch previewing the key colors and showing selection state through a highlighted border. Then I'll integrate it into the settings page by replacing the existing radio group with this new component.

But I need to reconcile the hook API change—the settings page currently uses `{ theme, setTheme, resolvedTheme }` where `resolvedTheme` is just `"light" | "dark"`, while the new hook would return `{ themePreference, setThemePreference, resolvedTheme }` with `resolvedTheme` as a full theme definition. Since the settings page is the only consumer, I should just update it to use the new API rather than maintaining backward compatibility. I'm leaning toward making ThemeSelector fully self-contained so it handles the hook internally and doesn't need props, which means the settings page just renders the component without managing theme state itself. I need to figure out where to place the ThemeProvider in the existing architecture since there's no React Context setup for theming yet.

input.prompt:
Design a detailed implementation plan for adding a custom theme system to the T3 Code editor. Here's everything I know about the codebase:

## CONTEXT

**Constraint:** Do NOT modify the build configuration or add new dependencies.

### Existing theme system (`apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts`):
- Uses module-level external store pattern with `useSyncExternalStore`
- Handles `"light" | "dark" | "system"` preferences
- Applies `.dark` class on `<html>` to toggle Tailwind dark variant
- Persists to localStorage key `t3code:theme`
- Applies theme immediately on module load to prevent flash

### Existing CSS (`apps/web/src/index.css`):
- Uses Tailwind CSS 4 with `@theme inline` block that maps Tailwind color utilities to CSS vars
- CSS custom properties defined on `:root` (light defaults) with `@variant dark` override block
- The `@custom-variant dark (&:is(.dark, .dark *))` defines dark mode via `.dark` class
- All components use semantic classes like `bg-background`, `text-foreground`, `bg-card`, etc.

### Existing settings page (`apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx`):
- Has an "Appearance" section with a radio group for light/dark/system
- Only consumer of `useTheme()` hook
- Uses `const { theme, setTheme, resolvedTheme } = useTheme()`

### Package architecture:
- `packages/shared`: subpath exports pattern (`./model`, `./git`, etc.), no barrel index, direct TS sources, runtime utilities
- `packages/contracts`: barrel exports, Effect Schema types only
- `apps/web`: React 19, Tailwind CSS 4, Zustand, TanStack Router. Uses `useSyncExternalStore` pattern for cross-component state without React Context (see `appSettings.ts` for the exact pattern)

## TASK REQUIREMENTS

1. Define a **theme type** in `packages/shared` with at least 3 built-in themes: `"t3-dark"`, `"t3-light"`, `"t3-midnight"`, covering colors for: background, foreground, accent, sidebar, editor, terminal, and status bar.

2. Create a **theme provider** and **`useTheme` hook** in the web app that:
   - Persists selection to localStorage
   - Applies CSS custom properties to the document

3. Add a **theme selector component** with preview swatches.

## DESIGN DECISIONS TO MAKE

1. **Where do color values live?** Options:
   - (A) In CSS (`:root[data-theme="t3-dark"]` blocks) - JS just sets attribute
   - (B) In TypeScript objects in `packages/shared/src/theme.ts` - JS applies via `style.setProperty()`
   - Option B aligns better with the requirement that the theme type is in shared package

2. **How to handle "system" preference?** Map "system" → resolves to t3-light (OS light) or t3-dark (OS dark)

3. **Migration from old `"light"/"dark"/"system"` values?** Use new localStorage key `t3code:color-theme`, migrate old key values: `"dark"` → `"t3-dark"`, `"light"` → `"t3-light"`, `"system"` → `"system"`

4. **ThemeProvider design?** Should be a React Context provider wrapping the app tree that also provides context for the resolved theme. Keep the `useSyncExternalStore` mechanism (external store pattern, not context-subscribed) for `useTheme()` to avoid re-renders.

5. **New CSS variables for sidebar/editor/terminal/statusBar?** Add them to the `@theme inline` block in `index.css` so they're usable as Tailwind utilities (`bg-sidebar`, etc.), and add fallback `:root` CSS values pointing to existing variables.

## PRODUCE

A detailed step-by-step plan covering:
1. Exact file list (new files + modified files) 
2. For each file, exact content structure/pseudocode
3. Concrete color values for each theme (as hex/oklch/rgba strings for use in TypeScript)
4. TypeScript interface shapes
5. How all pieces connect
6. Verification steps

The plan should be complete enough to implement without further research.

tool_use_result.prompt:
Design a detailed implementation plan for adding a custom theme system to the T3 Code editor. Here's everything I know about the codebase:

## CONTEXT

**Constraint:** Do NOT modify the build configuration or add new dependencies.

### Existing theme system (`apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts`):
- Uses module-level external store pattern with `useSyncExternalStore`
- Handles `"light" | "dark" | "system"` preferences
- Applies `.dark` class on `<html>` to toggle Tailwind dark variant
- Persists to localStorage key `t3code:theme`
- Applies theme immediately on module load to prevent flash

### Existing CSS (`apps/web/src/index.css`):
- Uses Tailwind CSS 4 with `@theme inline` block that maps Tailwind color utilities to CSS vars
- CSS custom properties defined on `:root` (light defaults) with `@variant dark` override block
- The `@custom-variant dark (&:is(.dark, .dark *))` defines dark mode via `.dark` class
- All components use semantic classes like `bg-background`, `text-foreground`, `bg-card`, etc.

### Existing settings page (`apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx`):
- Has an "Appearance" section with a radio group for light/dark/system
- Only consumer of `useTheme()` hook
- Uses `const { theme, setTheme, resolvedTheme } = useTheme()`

### Package architecture:
- `packages/shared`: subpath exports pattern (`./model`, `./git`, etc.), no barrel index, direct TS sources, runtime utilities
- `packages/contracts`: barrel exports, Effect Schema types only
- `apps/web`: React 19, Tailwind CSS 4, Zustand, TanStack Router. Uses `useSyncExternalStore` pattern for cross-component state without React Context (see `appSettings.ts` for the exact pattern)

## TASK REQUIREMENTS

1. Define a **theme type** in `packages/shared` with at least 3 built-in themes: `"t3-dark"`, `"t3-light"`, `"t3-midnight"`, covering colors for: background, foreground, accent, sidebar, editor, terminal, and status bar.

2. Create a **theme provider** and **`useTheme` hook** in the web app that:
   - Persists selection to localStorage
   - Applies CSS custom properties to the document

3. Add a **theme selector component** with preview swatches.

## DESIGN DECISIONS TO MAKE

1. **Where do color values live?** Options:
   - (A) In CSS (`:root[data-theme="t3-dark"]` blocks) - JS just sets attribute
   - (B) In TypeScript objects in `packages/shared/src/theme.ts` - JS applies via `style.setProperty()`
   - Option B aligns better with the requirement that the theme type is in shared package

2. **How to handle "system" preference?** Map "system" → resolves to t3-light (OS light) or t3-dark (OS dark)

3. **Migration from old `"light"/"dark"/"system"` values?** Use new localStorage key `t3code:color-theme`, migrate old key values: `"dark"` → `"t3-dark"`, `"light"` → `"t3-light"`, `"system"` → `"system"`

4. **ThemeProvider design?** Should be a React Context provider wrapping the app tree that also provides context for the resolved theme. Keep the `useSyncExternalStore` mechanism (external store pattern, not context-subscribed) for `useTheme()` to avoid re-renders.

5. **New CSS variables for sidebar/editor/terminal/statusBar?** Add them to the `@theme inline` block in `index.css` so they're usable as Tailwind utilities (`bg-sidebar`, etc.), and add fallback `:root` CSS values pointing to existing variables.

## PRODUCE

A detailed step-by-step plan covering:
1. Exact file list (new files + modified files) 
2. For each file, exact content structure/pseudocode
3. Concrete color values for each theme (as hex/oklch/rgba strings for use in TypeScript)
4. TypeScript interface shapes
5. How all pieces connect
6. Verification steps

The plan should be complete enough to implement without further research.

4. New `ThemeSelector.tsx` component with swatches

# Custom Theme System for T3 Code ## Context The app has a basic light/dark/system toggle (`useTheme.ts`) that only flips a `.dark` class on ``. The task is to add a full named-theme system with at least 3 built-in themes (`t3-light`, `t3-dark`, `t3-midnight`) covering background, foreground, accent, sidebar, editor, terminal, and status bar surfaces. Theme definitions live in `packages/shared`, and the web app hook applies them as CSS custom properties. A theme selector with preview swatches replaces the existing Appearance radio group in Settings. **Constraint:** No new dependencies, no build configuration changes. --- ## Architecture **Key decisions:** - Color values live as TypeScript objects in `packages/shared/src/theme.ts` (satisfies "theme type in shared package"), **not** in CSS blocks. - The web hook applies all color tokens via `document.documentElement.style.setProperty()` — inline styles win over `:root` CSS, so existing fallbacks remain as FOUC guards. - Existing `useTheme.ts` is **left untouched** — it still manages `.dark` class for old consumers. The new hook also toggles `.dark` since `applyColorTheme` calls `classList.toggle("dark", isDark)`. - No React Context needed — follows the `useSyncExternalStore` external-store pattern already used in `useTheme.ts` and `appSettings.ts`. - New localStorage key: `t3code:color-theme`. Migration on first read: old `"dark"` → `"t3-dark"`, `"light"` → `"t3-light"`, `"system"` → `"system"`. Old key is **not deleted** (existing `useTheme.ts` still reads it). - `ThemeProvider` is a thin component wrapping `__root.tsx` that calls `useColorTheme()` once to ensure subscription is live on mount. --- ## Files to Create / Modify ### 1. NEW — `packages/shared/src/theme.ts` Exports: - `interface ThemeColors` — all color token fields (28 properties) - `interface ColorThemeDefinition` — `{ id, label, isDark, colors }` - `type BuiltInColorTheme = "t3-light" | "t3-dark" | "t3-midnight"` - `type ColorThemePreference = BuiltInColorTheme | "system"` - `const BUILT_IN_COLOR_THEMES: BuiltInColorTheme[]` — ordered array for iteration - `const COLOR_THEME_DEFINITIONS: Record` — all 3 theme objects - `function resolveColorTheme(preference, systemIsDark): BuiltInColorTheme` — resolves "system" **ThemeColors fields:** ``` background, foreground, card, cardForeground, popover, popoverForeground, primary, primaryForeground, secondary, secondaryForeground, muted, mutedForeground, accent, accentForeground, destructive, destructiveForeground, border, input, ring, sidebar, sidebarForeground, sidebarAccent, sidebarAccentForeground, sidebarBorder, sidebarRing, sidebarPrimary, sidebarPrimaryForeground, editor, editorForeground, terminal, terminalForeground, statusBar, statusBarForeground ``` **Concrete color values:** `t3-light` (matches existing light `:root` palette): ``` background: "#ffffff" foreground: "#292d33" card: "#ffffff" cardForeground: "#292d33" popover: "#ffffff" popoverForeground: "#292d33" primary: "oklch(0.488 0.217 264)" primaryForeground: "#ffffff" secondary: "rgba(0,0,0,0.04)" secondaryForeground: "#292d33" muted: "rgba(0,0,0,0.04)" mutedForeground: "#737880" accent: "rgba(0,0,0,0.04)" accentForeground: "#292d33" destructive: "#ef4444" destructiveForeground: "#b91c1c" border: "rgba(0,0,0,0.08)" input: "rgba(0,0,0,0.10)" ring: "oklch(0.488 0.217 264)" sidebar: "#f5f5f7" sidebarForeground: "#292d33" sidebarAccent: "rgba(0,0,0,0.06)" sidebarAccentForeground: "#292d33" sidebarBorder: "rgba(0,0,0,0.08)" sidebarRing: "oklch(0.488 0.217 264)" sidebarPrimary: "oklch(0.488 0.217 264)" sidebarPrimaryForeground: "#ffffff" editor: "#ffffff" editorForeground: "#292d33" terminal: "#ffffff" terminalForeground: "#292d33" statusBar: "oklch(0.488 0.217 264)" statusBarForeground: "#ffffff" ``` `t3-dark` (matches existing dark `@variant dark` palette): ``` background: "#0c0f14" foreground: "#f1f3f7" card: "#0e1117" cardForeground: "#f1f3f7" popover: "#0e1117" popoverForeground: "#f1f3f7" primary: "oklch(0.588 0.217 264)" primaryForeground: "#ffffff" secondary: "rgba(255,255,255,0.04)" secondaryForeground: "#f1f3f7" muted: "rgba(255,255,255,0.04)" mutedForeground: "#737880" accent: "rgba(255,255,255,0.04)" accentForeground: "#f1f3f7" destructive: "#f16a7a" destructiveForeground: "#f87171" border: "rgba(255,255,255,0.06)" input: "rgba(255,255,255,0.08)" ring: "oklch(0.588 0.217 264)" sidebar: "#10141a" sidebarForeground: "#c8cdd6" sidebarAccent: "rgba(255,255,255,0.06)" sidebarAccentForeground: "#f1f3f7" sidebarBorder: "rgba(255,255,255,0.06)" sidebarRing: "oklch(0.588 0.217 264)" sidebarPrimary: "oklch(0.588 0.217 264)" sidebarPrimaryForeground: "#ffffff" editor: "#0c0f14" editorForeground: "#f1f3f7" terminal: "#090c11" terminalForeground: "#edf1f7" statusBar: "#0d1520" statusBarForeground: "#7ba4e8" ``` `t3-midnight` (deep navy/indigo, distinct personality): ``` background: "#060810" foreground: "#d4d8e8" card: "#0a0d18" cardForeground: "#d4d8e8" popover: "#0a0d18" popoverForeground: "#d4d8e8" primary: "oklch(0.640 0.190 248)" primaryForeground: "#ffffff" secondary: "rgba(100,120,200,0.08)" secondaryForeground: "#d4d8e8" muted: "rgba(100,120,200,0.07)" mutedForeground: "#6874a0" accent: "rgba(100,120,200,0.10)" accentForeground: "#d4d8e8" destructive: "#e05570" destructiveForeground: "#f87171" border: "rgba(100,120,200,0.10)" input: "rgba(100,120,200,0.12)" ring: "oklch(0.640 0.190 248)" sidebar: "#070a14" sidebarForeground: "#9aa3c8" sidebarAccent: "rgba(100,120,200,0.10)" sidebarAccentForeground: "#d4d8e8" sidebarBorder: "rgba(100,120,200,0.10)" sidebarRing: "oklch(0.640 0.190 248)" sidebarPrimary: "oklch(0.640 0.190 248)" sidebarPrimaryForeground: "#ffffff" editor: "#060810" editorForeground: "#d4d8e8" terminal: "#04060e" terminalForeground: "#cdd2e8" statusBar: "#090d1e" statusBarForeground: "#6e8fdd" ``` --- ### 2. MODIFY — `packages/shared/package.json` Add to the `"exports"` object (same pattern as existing `./model`, `./git` etc.): ```json "./theme": { "types": "./src/theme.ts", "import": "./src/theme.ts" } ``` --- ### 3. NEW — `apps/web/src/hooks/useColorTheme.ts` Follows the exact external-store pattern from the existing `useTheme.ts` and `appSettings.ts`. **Module-level state:** `listeners[]`, `lastSnapshot` (stable-reference optimization). **`getStoredPreference()`:** Reads `t3code:color-theme`. On first run (key absent), checks old `t3code:theme` key to migrate: `"dark"` → `"t3-dark"`, `"light"` → `"t3-light"`, `"system"` → `"system"`. Writes migrated value to new key. Old key left intact. **`applyColorTheme(preference, suppressTransitions?)`:** 1. Resolve preference → `BuiltInColorTheme` via `resolveColorTheme(preference, getSystemDark())` 2. Look up `COLOR_THEME_DEFINITIONS[resolvedId]` 3. Optionally add `.no-transitions` class 4. Set `data-color-theme` attribute on `document.documentElement` 5. Toggle `.dark` class based on `definition.isDark` 6. Call `root.style.setProperty(cssVar, value)` for all 34 color token CSS custom properties 7. Remove `.no-transitions` via `requestAnimationFrame` **CSS var name mapping** (camelCase → kebab-case with `--` prefix): - `cardForeground` → `--card-foreground` - `sidebarAccentForeground` → `--sidebar-accent-foreground` - `statusBar` → `--status-bar` - etc. **`applyColorTheme(getStoredPreference())`** — called at module level to prevent FOUC, same as existing `useTheme.ts`. **`subscribe(listener)`:** Registers media query listener (re-applies on OS dark mode change when preference is "system") and storage event listener (cross-tab sync on `t3code:color-theme` key change). **`useColorTheme()` hook:** ```typescript export function useColorTheme() { const snapshot = useSyncExternalStore(subscribe, getSnapshot); // resolve "system" → concrete id const resolvedColorTheme: BuiltInColorTheme = resolveColorTheme( snapshot.preference, snapshot.systemDark ); const setColorTheme = useCallback((next: ColorThemePreference) => { localStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, next); applyColorTheme(next, true); emitChange(); }, []); useEffect(() => { applyColorTheme(snapshot.preference); }, [snapshot.preference]); return { colorTheme: snapshot.preference, setColorTheme, resolvedColorTheme } as const; } ``` **Also export `applyColorTheme`** for future use (e.g., re-theming xterm in `ThreadTerminalDrawer.tsx`). --- ### 4. NEW — `apps/web/src/components/ThemeProvider.tsx` Thin component that integrates the theme system into the React tree. Its primary role is to ensure the subscription fires on mount and serves as the semantic "theme boundary" the task requires. ```typescript export function ThemeProvider({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) { useColorTheme(); // ensures subscription is active and DOM is kept in sync return <>{children}; } ``` --- ### 5. NEW — `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` **Props:** `{ value: ColorThemePreference; onChange: (next: ColorThemePreference) => void }` **Structure:** ```
← full-width row matching existing settings radio style
``` **`SystemThemeOption`:** Identical visual style to the existing settings radio rows (`border-primary/60 bg-primary/8` when selected). Label: "System", description: "Follows your OS appearance setting." **`ThemeCard`:** Each card (for a `BuiltInColorTheme`) shows: - A `64px` tall mini-preview panel with `background: colors.background` - Left strip `24px` wide: `background: colors.sidebar` (sidebar swatch) - Top bar inside content area `6px` tall: `background: colors.statusBar` - Two simulated text lines using `colors.border` at reduced opacity - Small circle accent dot: `background: colors.primary` - Theme label text below - Selected state: `ring-2 ring-primary/25 border-primary/60` - Hover state: `hover:border-primary/30` All colors applied via inline `style` props so they use the raw hex/oklch values from `COLOR_THEME_DEFINITIONS` (not the currently-active CSS variables) — this ensures swatches always show each theme's own palette regardless of which theme is active. --- ### 6. MODIFY — `apps/web/src/index.css` **Edit A — Inside `@theme inline { ... }`:** Add Tailwind utility mappings for new surface tokens (appended after the existing entries): ```css --color-sidebar: var(--sidebar); --color-sidebar-foreground: var(--sidebar-foreground); --color-sidebar-accent: var(--sidebar-accent); --color-sidebar-accent-foreground: var(--sidebar-accent-foreground); --color-sidebar-border: var(--sidebar-border); --color-sidebar-ring: var(--sidebar-ring); --color-sidebar-primary: var(--sidebar-primary); --color-sidebar-primary-foreground: var(--sidebar-primary-foreground); --color-editor: var(--editor); --color-editor-foreground: var(--editor-foreground); --color-terminal: var(--terminal); --color-terminal-foreground: var(--terminal-foreground); --color-status-bar: var(--status-bar); --color-status-bar-foreground: var(--status-bar-foreground); ``` **Edit B — Inside `:root { ... }` before `@variant dark`:** Add fallback values (active before JS runs): ```css --sidebar: var(--card); --sidebar-foreground: var(--foreground); --sidebar-accent: var(--accent); --sidebar-accent-foreground: var(--accent-foreground); --sidebar-border: var(--border); --sidebar-ring: var(--ring); --sidebar-primary: var(--primary); --sidebar-primary-foreground: var(--primary-foreground); --editor: var(--background); --editor-foreground: var(--foreground); --terminal: var(--background); --terminal-foreground: var(--foreground); --status-bar: var(--primary); --status-bar-foreground: var(--primary-foreground); ``` **Edit C — Inside `@variant dark { ... }`:** Dark-mode fallback overrides (same property names): ```css --sidebar: color-mix(in srgb, var(--background) 96%, var(--color-white)); --sidebar-foreground: var(--color-neutral-300); --sidebar-accent: --alpha(var(--color-white) / 6%); --sidebar-accent-foreground: var(--foreground); --sidebar-border: --alpha(var(--color-white) / 6%); --sidebar-ring: var(--ring); --sidebar-primary: var(--primary); --sidebar-primary-foreground: var(--primary-foreground); --editor: var(--background); --editor-foreground: var(--foreground); --terminal: color-mix(in srgb, var(--background) 90%, var(--color-black)); --terminal-foreground: var(--foreground); --status-bar: color-mix(in srgb, var(--primary) 20%, var(--background)); --status-bar-foreground: var(--primary); ``` --- ### 7. MODIFY — `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` **Remove:** The `THEME_OPTIONS` const array and the old radio group (`
...
`). **Add imports:** ```typescript import { useColorTheme } from "../hooks/useColorTheme"; import { ThemeSelector } from "../components/ThemeSelector"; ``` **In `SettingsRouteView`:** Replace `const { theme, setTheme, resolvedTheme } = useTheme()` with: ```typescript const { colorTheme, setColorTheme, resolvedColorTheme } = useColorTheme(); ``` (Remove the old `useTheme` import entirely if it's no longer used elsewhere in the file.) **Replace the Appearance section body** (the `
` block and the paragraph below it) with: ```tsx

Active theme:{" "} {COLOR_THEME_DEFINITIONS[resolvedColorTheme].label}

``` Also add `import { COLOR_THEME_DEFINITIONS } from "@t3tools/shared/theme"` to the imports. Update section description text to: "Choose a color theme for the editor." --- ### 8. MODIFY — `apps/web/src/routes/__root.tsx` Add import: ```typescript import { ThemeProvider } from "../components/ThemeProvider"; ``` In `RootRouteView`, wrap the returned JSX: ```tsx return ( ); ``` --- ## CSS Cascade Priority (why this works) 1. **`` (inline, set by `applyColorTheme`)** — wins over all stylesheet rules 2. **`@variant dark { --background: ... }` inside `:root`** — overrides base `:root`, overridden by #1 3. **`:root { --background: #ffffff }`** — base fallback, active only before JS runs (FOUC guard) --- ## Verification 1. **`bun typecheck`** from repo root — both `packages/shared` and `apps/web` must pass cleanly. The new `./theme` subpath export must resolve without errors. 2. **`bun lint`** — no new lint errors. 3. **FOUC test:** Hard reload in cold cache. The task is to add a full named-theme system with at least 3 built-in themes (`t3-light`, `t3-dark`, `t3-midnight`) covering background, foreground, accent, sidebar, editor, terminal, and status bar surfaces. Theme definitions live in `packages/shared`, and the web app hook applies them as CSS custom properties. A theme selector with preview swatches replaces the existing Appearance radio group in Settings. **Constraint:** No new dependencies, no build configuration changes. --- ## Architecture **Key decisions:** - Color values live as TypeScript objects in `packages/shared/src/theme.ts` (satisfies "theme type in shared package"), **not** in CSS blocks. - The web hook applies all color tokens via `document.documentElement.style.setProperty()` — inline styles win over `:root` CSS, so existing fallbacks remain as FOUC guards. - Existing `useTheme.ts` is **left untouched** — it still manages `.dark` class for old consumers. The new hook also toggles `.dark` since `applyColorTheme` calls `classList.toggle("dark", isDark)`. - No React Context needed — follows the `useSyncExternalStore` external-store pattern already used in `useTheme.ts` and `appSettings.ts`. - New localStorage key: `t3code:color-theme`. Migration on first read: old `"dark"` → `"t3-dark"`, `"light"` → `"t3-light"`, `"system"` → `"system"`. MODIFY — `packages/shared/package.json` Add to the `"exports"` object (same pattern as existing `./model`, `./git` etc.): ```json "./theme": { "types": "./src/theme.ts", "import": "./src/theme.ts" } ``` --- ### 3. NEW — `apps/web/src/hooks/useColorTheme.ts` Follows the exact external-store pattern from the existing `useTheme.ts` and `appSettings.ts`. **Module-level state:** `listeners[]`, `lastSnapshot` (stable-reference optimization). **`getStoredPreference()`:** Reads `t3code:color-theme`. On first run (key absent), checks old `t3code:theme` key to migrate: `"dark"` → `"t3-dark"`, `"light"` → `"t3-light"`, `"system"` → `"system"`. Writes migrated value to new key. Old key left intact. **`applyColorTheme(preference, suppressTransitions?)`:** 1. Resolve preference → `BuiltInColorTheme` via `resolveColorTheme(preference, getSystemDark())` 2. Look up `COLOR_THEME_DEFINITIONS[resolvedId]` 3. Optionally add `.no-transitions` class 4. Set `data-color-theme` attribute on `document.documentElement` 5. Toggle `.dark` class based on `definition.isDark` 6. Call `root.style.setProperty(cssVar, value)` for all 34 color token CSS custom properties 7. Remove `.no-transitions` via `requestAnimationFrame` **CSS var name mapping** (camelCase → kebab-case with `--` prefix): - `cardForeground` → `--card-foreground` - `sidebarAccentForeground` → `--sidebar-accent-foreground` - `statusBar` → `--status-bar` - etc. **`applyColorTheme(getStoredPreference())`** — called at module level to prevent FOUC, same as existing `useTheme.ts`. **`subscribe(listener)`:** Registers media query listener (re-applies on OS dark mode change when preference is "system") and storage event listener (cross-tab sync on `t3code:color-theme` key change). **`useColorTheme()` hook:** ```typescript export function useColorTheme() { const snapshot = useSyncExternalStore(subscribe, getSnapshot); // resolve "system" → concrete id const resolvedColorTheme: BuiltInColorTheme = resolveColorTheme( snapshot.preference, snapshot.systemDark ); const setColorTheme = useCallback((next: ColorThemePreference) => { localStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, next); applyColorTheme(next, true); emitChange(); }, []); useEffect(() => { applyColorTheme(snapshot.preference); }, [snapshot.preference]); return { colorTheme: snapshot.preference, setColorTheme, resolvedColorTheme } as const; } ``` **Also export `applyColorTheme`** for future use (e.g., re-theming xterm in `ThreadTerminalDrawer.tsx`). --- ### 4. NEW — `apps/web/src/components/ThemeProvider.tsx` Thin component that integrates the theme system into the React tree. Its primary role is to ensure the subscription fires on mount and serves as the semantic "theme boundary" the task requires. ```typescript export function ThemeProvider({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) { useColorTheme(); // ensures subscription is active and DOM is kept in sync return <>{children}; } ``` --- ### 5. NEW — `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` **Props:** `{ value: ColorThemePreference; onChange: (next: ColorThemePreference) => void }` **Structure:** ```
← full-width row matching existing settings radio style
``` **`SystemThemeOption`:** Identical visual style to the existing settings radio rows (`border-primary/60 bg-primary/8` when selected). Label: "System", description: "Follows your OS appearance setting." **`ThemeCard`:** Each card (for a `BuiltInColorTheme`) shows: - A `64px` tall mini-preview panel with `background: colors.background` - Left strip `24px` wide: `background: colors.sidebar` (sidebar swatch) - Top bar inside content area `6px` tall: `background: colors.statusBar` - Two simulated text lines using `colors.border` at reduced opacity - Small circle accent dot: `background: colors.primary` - Theme label text below - Selected state: `ring-2 ring-primary/25 border-primary/60` - Hover state: `hover:border-primary/30` All colors applied via inline `style` props so they use the raw hex/oklch values from `COLOR_THEME_DEFINITIONS` (not the currently-active CSS variables) — this ensures swatches always show each theme's own palette regardless of which theme is active. --- ### 6. MODIFY — `apps/web/src/index.css` **Edit A — Inside `@theme inline { ... }`:** Add Tailwind utility mappings for new surface tokens (appended after the existing entries): ```css --color-sidebar: var(--sidebar); --color-sidebar-foreground: var(--sidebar-foreground); --color-sidebar-accent: var(--sidebar-accent); --color-sidebar-accent-foreground: var(--sidebar-accent-foreground); --color-sidebar-border: var(--sidebar-border); --color-sidebar-ring: var(--sidebar-ring); --color-sidebar-primary: var(--sidebar-primary); --color-sidebar-primary-foreground: var(--sidebar-primary-foreground); --color-editor: var(--editor); --color-editor-foreground: var(--editor-foreground); --color-terminal: var(--terminal); --color-terminal-foreground: var(--terminal-foreground); --color-status-bar: var(--status-bar); --color-status-bar-foreground: var(--status-bar-foreground); ``` **Edit B — Inside `:root { ... }` before `@variant dark`:** Add fallback values (active before JS runs): ```css --sidebar: var(--card); --sidebar-foreground: var(--foreground); --sidebar-accent: var(--accent); --sidebar-accent-foreground: var(--accent-foreground); --sidebar-border: var(--border); --sidebar-ring: var(--ring); --sidebar-primary: var(--primary); --sidebar-primary-foreground: var(--primary-foreground); --editor: var(--background); --editor-foreground: var(--foreground); --terminal: var(--background); --terminal-foreground: var(--foreground); --status-bar: var(--primary); --status-bar-foreground: var(--primary-foreground); ``` **Edit C — Inside `@variant dark { ... }`:** Dark-mode fallback overrides (same property names): ```css --sidebar: color-mix(in srgb, var(--background) 96%, var(--color-white)); --sidebar-foreground: var(--color-neutral-300); --sidebar-accent: --alpha(var(--color-white) / 6%); --sidebar-accent-foreground: var(--foreground); --sidebar-border: --alpha(var(--color-white) / 6%); --sidebar-ring: var(--ring); --sidebar-primary: var(--primary); --sidebar-primary-foreground: var(--primary-foreground); --editor: var(--background); --editor-foreground: var(--foreground); --terminal: color-mix(in srgb, var(--background) 90%, var(--color-black)); --terminal-foreground: var(--foreground); --status-bar: color-mix(in srgb, var(--primary) 20%, var(--background)); --status-bar-foreground: var(--primary); ``` --- ### 7. MODIFY — `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` **Remove:** The `THEME_OPTIONS` const array and the old radio group (`
...
`). **Add imports:** ```typescript import { useColorTheme } from "../hooks/useColorTheme"; import { ThemeSelector } from "../components/ThemeSelector"; ``` **In `SettingsRouteView`:** Replace `const { theme, setTheme, resolvedTheme } = useTheme()` with: ```typescript const { colorTheme, setColorTheme, resolvedColorTheme } = useColorTheme(); ``` (Remove the old `useTheme` import entirely if it's no longer used elsewhere in the file.) **Replace the Appearance section body** (the `

``` Also add `import { COLOR_THEME_DEFINITIONS } from "@t3tools/shared/theme"` to the imports. Update section description text to: "Choose a color theme for the editor." --- ### 8. input.summary:
Here's the full plan for the custom theme system:

## What's Being Built

A named-theme system with 3 built-in themes (`t3-light`, `t3-dark`, `t3-midnight`) layered on top of the existing light/dark mechanism. Theme definitions live in `packages/shared` as typed TypeScript objects; the web hook applies them as CSS custom properties via `style.setProperty`.

## Key Design Decisions

- **Existing `useTheme.ts` untouched** — the new `useColorTheme.ts` is additive and parallel, following the identical `useSyncExternalStore` external-store pattern
- **Color values in TypeScript** (not CSS blocks) — satisfies "theme type in shared package" and makes them portable
- **`style.setProperty` wins** over `:root` CSS, so the CSS continues to serve as a FOUC guard before JS runs
- **New localStorage key** `t3code:color-theme` with auto-migration from old `t3code:theme` values
- **`t3-midnight`** is a distinct deep navy/indigo palette — not just another dark theme

## Files

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `packages/shared/src/theme.ts` | NEW — types + all 3 theme color objects |
| `packages/shared/package.json` | MODIFY — add `./theme` subpath export |
| `apps/web/src/hooks/useColorTheme.ts` | NEW — the hook + `applyColorTheme` |
| `apps/web/src/components/ThemeProvider.tsx` | NEW — thin React wrapper |
| `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` | NEW — grid of theme cards with swatches |
| `apps/web/src/index.css` | MODIFY — add `--sidebar`, `--editor`, `--terminal`, `--status-bar` vars to `@theme inline` + fallbacks |
| `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` | MODIFY — replace radio group with `<ThemeSelector />` |
| `apps/web/src/routes/__root.tsx` | MODIFY — add `<ThemeProvider>` wrapper |

No new dependencies. No build config changes.

content.text:
Here's the full plan for the custom theme system:

## What's Being Built

A named-theme system layered on top of the existing light/dark mechanism. Theme definitions live in `packages/shared` as typed TypeScript objects; the web hook applies them as CSS custom properties via `style.setProperty`.

## Key Design Decisions

- **Existing `useTheme.ts` left untouched** — the new `useColorTheme.ts` is additive, following the identical `useSyncExternalStore` external-store pattern
- **Color values in TypeScript, not CSS** — satisfies "theme type in shared package" and keeps definitions portable
- **CSS cascade:** inline `style.setProperty` beats `:root` CSS, so the existing CSS serves as a FOUC guard before JS runs
- **New localStorage key** `t3code:color-theme` with auto-migration from old `t3code:theme` values
- **`t3-midnight`** is a distinct deep navy/indigo palette, not a clone of `t3-dark`

## 8 Files

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `packages/shared/src/theme.ts` | **NEW** — interfaces + all 3 theme color objects |
| `packages/shared/package.json` | **MODIFY** — add `./theme` subpath export |
| `apps/web/src/hooks/useColorTheme.ts` | **NEW** — hook + `applyColorTheme` |
| `apps/web/src/components/ThemeProvider.tsx` | **NEW** — thin React wrapper (calls the hook on mount) |
| `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` | **NEW** — grid of theme cards with color swatches |
| `apps/web/src/index.css` | **MODIFY** — add `--sidebar`, `--editor`, `--terminal`, `--status-bar` vars to `@theme inline` + fallbacks |
| `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` | **MODIFY** — replace radio group with `<ThemeSelector />` |
| `apps/web/src/routes/__root.tsx` | **MODIFY** — add `<ThemeProvider>` wrapper |

No new dependencies. No build config changes.

tool_input.plan:
# Custom Theme System for T3 Code ## Context The app has a basic light/dark/system toggle (`useTheme.ts`) that only flips a `.dark` class on ``. The task is to add a full named-theme system with at least 3 built-in themes (`t3-light`, `t3-dark`, `t3-midnight`) covering background, foreground, accent, sidebar, editor, terminal, and status bar surfaces. Theme definitions live in `packages/shared`, and the web app hook applies them as CSS custom properties. A theme selector with preview swatches replaces the existing Appearance radio group in Settings. **Constraint:** No new dependencies, no build configuration changes. --- ## Architecture **Key decisions:** - Color values live as TypeScript objects in `packages/shared/src/theme.ts` (satisfies "theme type in shared package"), **not** in CSS blocks. - The web hook applies all color tokens via `document.documentElement.style.setProperty()` — inline styles win over `:root` CSS, so existing fallbacks remain as FOUC guards. - Existing `useTheme.ts` is **left untouched** — it still manages `.dark` class for old consumers. The new hook also toggles `.dark` since `applyColorTheme` calls `classList.toggle("dark", isDark)`. - No React Context needed — follows the `useSyncExternalStore` external-store pattern already used in `useTheme.ts` and `appSettings.ts`. - New localStorage key: `t3code:color-theme`. Migration on first read: old `"dark"` → `"t3-dark"`, `"light"` → `"t3-light"`, `"system"` → `"system"`. Old key is **not deleted** (existing `useTheme.ts` still reads it). - `ThemeProvider` is a thin component wrapping `__root.tsx` that calls `useColorTheme()` once to ensure subscription is live on mount. --- ## Files to Create / Modify ### 1. NEW — `packages/shared/src/theme.ts` Exports: - `interface ThemeColors` — all color token fields (28 properties) - `interface ColorThemeDefinition` — `{ id, label, isDark, colors }` - `type BuiltInColorTheme = "t3-light" | "t3-dark" | "t3-midnight"` - `type ColorThemePreference = BuiltInColorTheme | "system"` - `const BUILT_IN_COLOR_THEMES: BuiltInColorTheme[]` — ordered array for iteration - `const COLOR_THEME_DEFINITIONS: Record` — all 3 theme objects - `function resolveColorTheme(preference, systemIsDark): BuiltInColorTheme` — resolves "system" **ThemeColors fields:** ``` background, foreground, card, cardForeground, popover, popoverForeground, primary, primaryForeground, secondary, secondaryForeground, muted, mutedForeground, accent, accentForeground, destructive, destructiveForeground, border, input, ring, sidebar, sidebarForeground, sidebarAccent, sidebarAccentForeground, sidebarBorder, sidebarRing, sidebarPrimary, sidebarPrimaryForeground, editor, editorForeground, terminal, terminalForeground, statusBar, statusBarForeground ``` **Concrete color values:** `t3-light` (matches existing light `:root` palette): ``` background: "#ffffff" foreground: "#292d33" card: "#ffffff" cardForeground: "#292d33" popover: "#ffffff" popoverForeground: "#292d33" primary: "oklch(0.488 0.217 264)" primaryForeground: "#ffffff" secondary: "rgba(0,0,0,0.04)" secondaryForeground: "#292d33" muted: "rgba(0,0,0,0.04)" mutedForeground: "#737880" accent: "rgba(0,0,0,0.04)" accentForeground: "#292d33" destructive: "#ef4444" destructiveForeground: "#b91c1c" content.text:
All 8 files are done and lint passes clean. Here's a summary of everything implemented:

---

## What was implemented

### New files

**`packages/shared/src/theme.ts`** — The typed theme system foundation:
- `ThemeColors` interface — 34 color token fields covering core UI, sidebar, editor, terminal, and status bar surfaces
- `ColorThemeDefinition` interface — `{ id, label, isDark, colors }`
- `BuiltInColorTheme` type (`"t3-light" | "t3-dark" | "t3-midnight"`)
- `ColorThemePreference` type (adds `"system"`)
- `COLOR_THEME_DEFINITIONS` constant with all 3 fully-specified theme objects
- `resolveColorTheme()` helper that maps `"system"` → `"t3-dark"` (OS dark) or `"t3-light"` (OS light)

**`apps/web/src/hooks/useColorTheme.ts`** — Follows the identical `useSyncExternalStore` external-store pattern used throughout the codebase:
- Applies theme immediately on module load (FOUC prevention)
- Migrates from old `t3code:theme` key values on first run
- Sets all 34 CSS custom properties via `style.setProperty` on `<html>` (highest cascade priority)
- Cross-tab sync via `storage` event; OS preference sync via `matchMedia` listener
- Exports `applyColorTheme` for future use (e.g., xterm re-theming)

**`apps/web/src/components/ThemeProvider.tsx`** — Thin React wrapper that ensures the hook subscription is live on mount; placed at the root of the component tree.

**`apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx`** — Theme picker with mini app-chrome swatch previews: a full-width "System" row + a 3-column grid of `ThemeCard` components. Each card shows the theme's own `background`, `sidebar`, `statusBar`, `border`, and `primary` colors via inline styles (so swatches are always accurate regardless of the active theme).

### Modified files

**`packages/shared/package.json`** — Added `./theme` subpath export following the same pattern as `./model`, `./git`, etc.

**`apps/web/src/index.css`** — Three additions:
1. New `--color-sidebar-*`, `--color-editor-*`, `--color-terminal-*`, `--color-status-bar-*` mappings in `@theme inline` (enabling `bg-sidebar`, `text-sidebar-foreground`, etc. as Tailwind utilities)
2. Light-mode fallback values in `:root` (point to existing vars so nothing breaks before JS runs)
3. Dark-mode fallback values in `@variant dark`

**`apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx`** — Replaced the old light/dark/system radio group with `<ThemeSelector />`, wired to `useColorTheme`.

**`apps/web/src/routes/__root.tsx`** — Added `<ThemeProvider>` as the outermost wrapper in `RootRouteView`.

---

## What was implemented

### New files

**`packages/shared/src/theme.ts`** — The typed theme system foundation:
- `ThemeColors` interface — 34 color token fields covering core UI, sidebar, editor, terminal, and status bar surfaces
- `ColorThemeDefinition` interface — `{ id, label, isDark, colors }`
- `BuiltInColorTheme` type (`"t3-light" | "t3-dark" | "t3-midnight"`)
- `ColorThemePreference` type (adds `"system"`)
- `COLOR_THEME_DEFINITIONS` constant with all 3 fully-specified theme objects
- `resolveColorTheme()` helper that maps `"system"` → `"t3-dark"` (OS dark) or `"t3-light"` (OS light)

**`apps/web/src/hooks/useColorTheme.ts`** — Follows the identical `useSyncExternalStore` external-store pattern used throughout the codebase:
- Applies theme immediately on module load (FOUC prevention)
- Migrates from old `t3code:theme` key values on first run
- Sets all 34 CSS custom properties via `style.setProperty` on `<html>` (highest cascade priority)
- Cross-tab sync via `storage` event; OS preference sync via `matchMedia` listener
- Exports `applyColorTheme` for future use (e.g., xterm re-theming)

**`apps/web/src/components/ThemeProvider.tsx`** — Thin React wrapper that ensures the hook subscription is live on mount; placed at the root of the component tree.

**`apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx`** — Theme picker with mini app-chrome swatch previews: a full-width "System" row + a 3-column grid of `ThemeCard` components. Each card shows the theme's own `background`, `sidebar`, `statusBar`, `border`, and `primary` colors via inline styles (so swatches are always accurate regardless of the active theme).

### Modified files

**`packages/shared/package.json`** — Added `./theme` subpath export following the same pattern as `./model`, `./git`, etc.

**`apps/web/src/index.css`** — Three additions:
1. New `--color-sidebar-*`, `--color-editor-*`, `--color-terminal-*`, `--color-status-bar-*` mappings in `@theme inline` (enabling `bg-sidebar`, `text-sidebar-foreground`, etc. as Tailwind utilities)
2. Light-mode fallback values in `:root` (point to existing vars so nothing breaks before JS runs)
3. Dark-mode fallback values in `@variant dark`

**`apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx`** — Replaced the old light/dark/system radio group with `<ThemeSelector />`, wired to `useColorTheme`.

**`apps/web/src/routes/__root.tsx`** — Added `<ThemeProvider>` as the outermost wrapper in `RootRouteView`.
